decision_question: Can three bounded critical-fix iterations make TEMPO implementation authority failure-atomic, start-bound, repository-bound, and regression-tested without weakening human authorization?
initiating_context: Dogfooding TEMPO while building Understand Video exposed a split-state start failure and an authorization-only write path. The repository owner requested three framework-first stabilization rounds before the dependent product is finalized.

target_user.segment: Repository owners supervising coding agents with TEMPO
target_user.description: Maintainers who rely on TEMPO to distinguish business readiness, human authorization, and a committed bounded implementation start.
target_user.usage_context: Before an agent writes implementation files and while reviewers inspect machine receipts after failures or restarts.

economic_buyer.description: The repository owner bears the security and review cost of an authority bypass or ambiguous audit record.
economic_buyer.value_recipient: The owner and implementing team receive a fail-closed, inspectable build lease and reproducible recovery behavior.

job_to_be_done: Ensure that implementation writes are allowed only after one matching, committed mvp_started event and that failed lifecycle operations leave no authoritative partial state.
observed_problem: The current implementation mutates active/state before appending mvp_started, and status plus guards can treat a warrant as build authority before a committed start exists.
current_alternatives: Rely on authorization alone and manually check whether start completed
Clean up partial state by hand after a failed command
Stop using the framework gate for development
expected_value: A stable framework contract that independent products can consume without duplicating lifecycle checks or coupling their code to TEMPO internals.
strategic_context: This is a post-freeze critical release fix. The work must preserve separate human authorization, signed history, exit-code semantics, and repository independence while adding regression evidence for three iterations.

constraints.description: No change may weaken a gate, fabricate human approval, rewrite signed history, or convert an absent start into build authority.
Lifecycle writes must be failure-atomic and guards must bind state, active lease, ledger event, warrant, task, lane, action, path, and subject repository.
Exactly three evidence-driven framework iterations precede their matching Understand Video product iterations.
The stabilization and dependent Devpost delivery review must finish before the Build Week submission deadline.
The framework stabilization has a zero-dollar external-service budget.
